description: BloodHound is a single page JavaScript web application, built on top of Linkurious, compiled with Electron, with a Neo4j database fed by data from a Powershell ingestor.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `cd toolboxes/bloodhound && python3 bloodhound.py --target $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST`. Further invocations from upstream: `cd toolboxes/bloodhound && python3 bloodhound.py --help`; `cd toolboxes/bloodhound && python3 bloodhound.py --help`. AD attack-path mapper (Python client).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the post_exploitation category. Fits when the engagement needs: AD attack-path mapper (Python client).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: AD attack-path mapper (Python client).

name: bloodhound

trust.notes: Operator-curated Python library. The pip name is resolved from PyPI; the import name is verified against the registry.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
